notes: First-question data regime assertion fails for BOTH with_skill and without_skill Socratic runs — both advisors open with problem clarification before asking about evaluation cost. The skill gets there by Turn 3, which is reasonable but doesn't satisfy 'first substantive question'.

Numbered reference format [1],[2] fails in thermal-monitoring with_skill body text (uses APA inline) and all without_skill runs — the report_compiler_agent is not enforcing inline numbered citations consistently.

thermal-monitoring with_skill organized by sensor/modality type across 8 families rather than by ML method family — bibliography_agent is grouping by application domain. The skill's model-family instruction needs to be stricter about what constitutes a 'family' (shared ML assumption, not shared sensor).
ml-process-control with_skill achieved 6/6 — the full mode pipeline with DA checkpoints produces the most complete output. This is the strongest result.

Without-skill baseline is surprisingly strong on temporal methods (b1, b2, b5) — Claude already knows AM well. The skill's marginal value is in structured organization (assumption tracing, engineering baseline framing, model-family grouping, sim-to-real audit). The 0/6 on thermal-monitoring without_skill confirms this: no framework = no structure.
Socratic without_skill scored 4/6 — better than expected for a baseline. Gap is on sim-to-real scope question (c2) which the skill correctly asks. First-question issue is a tie.
